summary: Pool 1 Routing Fix Testing Complete - All 17 backend tests passed (100%). Pool 1 now correctly includes BOTH Partners AND Internal Staff. Fixed a bug in notify_pool_users() where 'vendor' variable was used instead of 'user'.
passed_tests: test_01_internal_staff_user_exists_with_correct_account_type - Internal Staff user verified with account_type='Internal Staff'
test_05_pool_2_access_for_internal_staff_operator - Internal Staff (Operator role) can access Pool 2 as expected
test_06_pool_2_access_for_partner_operator - Partner (Operator role) can access Pool 2 as expected
test_07_order_rrg_000099_routed_to_pool_1 - RRG-000099 correctly routed to POOL_1 (Internal Staff has matching specialty)
test_08_order_rrg_000100_routed_to_pool_2 - RRG-000100 correctly routed to POOL_2 (no Pool 1 users with matching specialty)
test_09_internal_staff_sees_matching_specialty_tickets_in_pool_1 - Internal Staff sees tickets matching their specialty in Pool 1
test_10_admin_can_see_all_pool_1_tickets - Admin can access all Pool 1 tickets
test_11_admin_can_see_all_pool_2_tickets - Admin can access all Pool 2 tickets
test_12_verify_pool_1_query_includes_both_account_types - Pool 1 eligible users include both Partners and Internal Staff
test_15_pool_1_ticket_has_24h_expiry - Pool 1 tickets have pool1_expires_at set
test_16_pool_2_ticket_has_no_expiry - Pool 2 tickets (skipped Pool 1) have no pool1_expires_at
test_17_verify_routing_specialty_is_set - Tickets have routing_specialty_id and routing_specialty_name set
critical_code_review_comments: FIXED: Bug in notify_pool_users() line 384-385 - used 'vendor' variable instead of 'user' for Pool 2 email notifications
Pool 1 query correctly uses {account_type: {$in: ['Partner', 'Internal Staff']}} in determine_pool_routing()
Access control for /api/orders/pool/1 correctly allows account_type in ['Partner', 'Internal Staff']
Pool 1 tickets have pool1_expires_at set (24h from creation)
Pool 2 tickets (skipped Pool 1) correctly have pool1_expires_at=None
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_pool1_routing_fix.py - New comprehensive test file for Pool 1 routing fix
/app/backend/routes/orders.py - Fixed bug in notify_pool_users() (line 384-385: vendor -> user)
seed_data_creation: Test orders RRG-000099 (Pool 1 via Internal Staff) and RRG-000100 (Pool 2 direct) already exist
context_for_next_testing_agent: Pool 1 routing fix verified. Pool 1 now includes BOTH Partners AND Internal Staff. Key changes: 1) determine_pool_routing() uses $in: ['Partner', 'Internal Staff'] for Pool 1 candidates, 2) get_pool_tickets() access control allows account_type in ['Partner', 'Internal Staff'] for Pool 1, 3) Fixed bug in notify_pool_users() where 'vendor' was used instead of 'user'. Test orders: RRG-000099 (Pool 1 - Administrative Assistant specialty), RRG-000100 (Pool 2 - no Pool 1 users for specialty).

ticket_routing_to_pool_1.example: RRG-000099 routed to POOL_1 (Internal Staff has Administrative Assistant specialty)

ticket_skip_pool_1_to_pool_2.example: RRG-000100 routed directly to POOL_2 (no Pool 1 users with matching specialty)

mocked_apis.note: GoHighLevel payment webhook is MOCKED as per previous context
